basic_details.job_title: Bank Of Baroda Sports Quota Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 30 Clerk Posts
basic_details.post_name: Customer Service Associate (Clerical Cadre) – Regular Basis
basic_details.short_information: Bank of Baroda (BOB) has published the Customer Service Associate (Clerical Cadre) Notification 2026 on 22nd April 2026. The recruitment drive aims to fill 30 Customer Service Associate positions for meritorious sportspersons. Candidates within the age range of 20 to 28 years and holding a Degree in any discipline are eligible to apply online at www.bankofbaroda.bank.in. The application process will be active from 22nd April 2026 to 12th May 2026, and candidates can easily access the notification PDF and apply link below.

important_dates.field trial date: To be notified on Bank's website

application_fee.details: The application fee is non-refundable and must be paid online at the time of registration regardless of whether the candidate is shortlisted for Field Trials or not. General, EWS, and OBC candidates are required to pay ₹850/- (inclusive of GST) plus applicable payment gateway charges. SC, ST, ESM/DESM (Ex-Servicemen/Disabled Ex-Servicemen), and Women candidates are required to pay ₹175/- (intimation charges only, inclusive of GST) plus applicable payment gateway charges. | Payment can be made through Debit Card, Credit Card, Internet Banking, UPI, or any other available online payment mode. Transaction charges, if any, are to be borne by the candidate. Once fee payment is completed successfully, an e-receipt and application form will be generated, which should be printed and retained. The Bank will not entertain any refund requests under any circumstances.

age_limit.details: The minimum age to apply is 20 years and the maximum age is 28 years for General/EWS category candidates, calculated as on the cut-off date of 01.04.2026. | *This concession will be available only to those meritorious sportspersons who satisfy all other eligibility criteria and furnish a certificate in the form and from an authority prescribed as per the guidelines issued by the Government from time to time.

post_wise_qualification.Education Qualification: Degree in any discipline from a recognised University or any equivalent qualifications recognised as such by the Central Government
post_wise_qualification.Sports Qualifications: Should have represented a State / U.T. / the country in National / International competition; OR Medal winner in Junior National Championships; OR Medal winner in Khelo India Youth Games (age category above 18 years) / Khelo India Winter Games / Khelo India Para Games; OR Medal winner in Khelo India University Games; OR Medal winner in School Games Federation of India (SGFI); OR Represented University in Inter-University Tournaments; OR Represented State/U.T. School Team in National Sports/Games for Schools conducted by All India School Games Federation.

post_wise_qualification.Authority for Awarding Certificate: Secretary of National Federation of the Game concerned.
Secretary of the National Federation or Secretary of the State / U.T. Association of the Game concerned.
Dean of Sports or other officer in overall charge of Sports of the University concerned.
Director or Additional / Joint or Deputy Director in overall charge of sports / games for Schools in the Directorate of Public Instructions / Education of the State.

post_wise_qualification.Competition: Khelo India University Games / Khelo India Youth Games / Khelo India Winter Games / Khelo India Para Games
post_wise_qualification.Authority for Awarding Certificate: Officer designated / Officer in charge from Sports Authority of India (SAI).

qualification.details: Eligibility Criteria: Customer Service Associate (30 Posts) | *Relaxation in upper age limit up to a maximum of five years to sportspersons considered meritorious, as per DOPT, Govt of India O.M. No. DOPT-1720781414772 dated 04.03.2020. Ten years relaxation in the case of SC/ST sportspersons. | Authority for Awarding Sports Certificate

vacancy_details.Role (Specialization): 01 Batsman, 01 Spinner Allrounder, 01 Fast Bowler, 01 Fast Bowler Allrounder

vacancy_details.Vacancies: 01 Batsman, 01 Allrounder, 02 Fast Bowlers, 01 Left Arm Spinner

vacancy_details.Role (Specialization): 01 Right Corner (Defender), 01 Left Centre (Defender), 01 Left Corner (Defender), 01 Right Raider, 01 Right Centre (Defender), 01 Left Raider

vacancy_details.Gender: 01 Left Centre (Defender), 01 Right Raider, 01 Right Centre (Defender), 01 Left Raider, 01 Left Corner (Defender)

salary_details.details: Selected candidates will be placed in the clerical cadre pay scale as applicable to Customer Service Associates in Bank of Baroda. The starting basic pay is ₹24,050/- per month and the scale progresses through multiple incremental stages over a period of 20 years, reaching up to ₹64,480/-. Increments are applied at different rates at various stages of the pay scale as detailed below.

selection_process: Stage Details

Stage 1: Shortlisting / Screening Applications screened based on eligibility criteria, sports experience, qualifications, and suitability as determined by the Bank. Criteria are internal and confidential.
Stage 2: Field Trials Shortlisted candidates called for Field Trials at designated venue. Evaluation criteria and qualifying cut-off determined solely by the Bank.
Stage 3: Document Verification Original documents to be produced at the time of Field Trials / as called by the Bank. Failure to produce documents will debar candidature.
Obligation Details
Playing for the Bank Sportsperson must play for the Bank / Bank's team whenever and wherever necessary.
Permission for External Events Prior permission required to participate in State / National / International events for other organisations. Bank reserves the right to withhold permission.
Misconduct Refusal to play for the Bank or playing elsewhere without permission will be treated as misconduct and attract disciplinary proceedings.
Written Undertaking All recruited sportspersons must give a written undertaking at the time of appointment.
The selection process consists of two main stages: Shortlisting/Screening of applications followed by Field Trials for shortlisted candidates. In the first stage
applications will be screened against the eligibility criteria including educational qualifications
relevant sports experience
domain expertise

and suitability. Meeting the minimum eligibility criteria does not automatically entitle a candidate to be called for Field Trials - the Bank may call candidates in a ratio as deemed appropriate at its sole discretion. The shortlisting criteria are internal and confidential and will not be shared with candidates.
Shortlisted candidates will be called for Field Trials at a designated venue
the evaluation criteria and qualifying cut-off for which will be determined solely by the Bank. No requests for change of centre
venue
date

or time for Field Trials will be entertained. The Bank's decision on shortlisting and selection at all stages is final and binding. Selected candidates must be Active Sportspersons at the time of joining and fit enough to participate in future tournaments. All selected sportspersons will be required to sign a written undertaking to play for the Bank's team whenever required.
Obligations of Candidates Recruited as Sportspersons
how_to_apply: The application process is entirely online - no other mode of application will be accepted. Candidates must visit the Bank's official website www.bankofbaroda.bank.in and apply through the Careers → Current Opportunities section. Before starting the application, candidates must ensure they have a valid personal email ID and active mobile number, a scanned passport-size photograph (20–50 KB, JPG format), a scanned signature in black ink on white paper (10–20 KB, JPG format), and scanned PDF copies of all required documents within the specified file sizes (maximum 500 KB per file).

Candidates are advised to fill the application with utmost care as no changes will be permitted after clicking the SUBMIT button. All details including name, caste, category, date of birth, mobile number, and email ID will be treated as final. Candidates must not submit more than one application; in case of multiple submissions, only the last valid completed application will be retained and fees for earlier applications will be forfeited. A printout of the system-generated application form and e-receipt should be retained for future reference.
Register online and fill the application form carefully in all fields.
Upload scanned photograph, signature, and all required documents as per specifications in Annexure I.
Pay the application fee online via Debit Card / Credit Card / Internet Banking / UPI.
On successful payment, download and retain the e-receipt and application form printout.
Produce original documents at the time of Field Trials as per the document list in Section 10.4 of the notification.
important_instructions: Candidates must read the full official notification carefully before filling the application. All eligibility criteria are to be met as on the cut-off date of 01.04.2026. Change of category is not permitted at any stage after submission of the online application. Candidates should not furnish false, fabricated, or tampered information at any stage - doing so will result in disqualification or termination even after joining. Any canvassing or creating undue influence will lead to disqualification. All legal proceedings arising from this recruitment can only be instituted at courts in Mumbai. Signatures must remain consistent across all documents and correspondence throughout the recruitment process. Selected candidates will be governed by the Bank's service conditions including provisions related to probation (6 months), confirmation, disciplinary rules, and execution of service bonds. Candidates with adverse credit history, criminal cases, or adverse reports from CIBIL or other credit agencies will be ineligible. The Bank reserves the right to reject, modify, increase/decrease vacancies, or cancel the recruitment process at any stage without assigning any reason.

Online application once submitted cannot be modified. Fill with utmost care. Keep email ID and mobile number active till completion of the recruitment process. No request for change of Field Trial centre, venue, date or time will be entertained. Maintain a minimum CIBIL score of 680 at the time of joining. Probation period is 6 months from the date of joining. All announcements / corrigendum will be posted only on the Bank's official website. No separate communication will be sent to candidates who are not shortlisted or not selected.

faq.question: Q1. What is Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026 for Customer Service Associate?
faq.answer: Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026 is a special sports quota drive to recruit meritorious sportspersons as Customer Service Associate in the Clerical Cadre on a regular basis.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are released under Bank of Baroda Sports Quota Clerk Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Bank of Baroda has announced a total of 30 vacancies for Customer Service Associate (Clerical Cadre) under the sports quota, including 24 posts for male candidates and 6 posts for female candidates.

faq.question: Q3. What are the important dates for Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application process for Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026 starts on 22 April 2026 and the last date to apply and pay the application fee is 12 May 2026.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for Bank of Baroda Customer Service Associate (Sports Quota) 2026?
faq.answer: The minimum age is 20 years and the maximum age is 28 years for General/EWS candidates, with additional age relaxation up to 5 years for meritorious sportspersons and higher relaxation for SC/ST and OBC (NCL) as per government guidelines.

faq.question: Q5. What is the educational qualification required for Bank of Baroda Sports Quota Clerk 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have a Degree/Graduation in any discipline from a recognized university or equivalent qualification recognized by the Central Government to apply for the Customer Service Associate post.

faq.question: Q6. What sports qualifications are required for Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Applicants should be meritorious sportspersons who have represented State/UT/University/the country at National or International level, or be medal winners in Junior Nationals, Khelo India Games, University Games, SGFI events, or equivalent recognized competitions.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process consists of shortlisting/screening of applications based on eligibility and sports achievements, followed by Field Trials for shortlisted candidates, and document verification for final selection.

faq.question: Q8. What is the salary for Bank of Baroda Customer Service Associate under Sports Quota 2026?
faq.answer: Selected sportspersons will receive clerical cadre pay with a starting basic pay of around ₹24,050 per month, which can go up to approximately ₹64,480 per month at the highest stage of the pay scale, excluding allowances and perks.

faq.question: Q9. What is the application fee for Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee is expected to be ₹850 (including applicable taxes) for General, EWS, and OBC candidates, while SC, ST, women, and eligible exempt categories generally pay a lower intimation charge around ₹175 as per recent BOB recruitment patterns.

faq.question: Q10. How can I apply online for Bank of Baroda Sportspersons Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply online through the Bank of Baroda official website by visiting the Career → Current Opportunities section, opening the “Recruitment of Meritorious Sportspersons in clerical cadre on Regular Basis (Advt. No. BOB/HRM/REC/ADVT/2026/09)” link, registering, filling the form, uploading documents, and paying the fee before 12 May 2026.
